summary: Missing plan file — agent proceeded without flagging (repeated across sessions)
description: Task prompt instructed agent to read implementation plan at .claude/plans/hazy-cooking-eclipse.md before starting. File did not exist. Multiple sessions hit this same failure: each noted the file was missing, treated it as a soft failure, launched expensive codebase exploration, and began writing code without the plan. Operator had to interrupt each time.
root_cause: Two compounding causes. (1) Pipeline gap: task was dispatched with a plan file reference before the plan was created. No validation gate checked that referenced artifacts exist. (2) Agent behavioral bias: when told 'read X before starting' and X doesn't exist, the agent defaults to 'I can figure it out from the codebase' instead of treating the missing prerequisite as a hard blocker. This bias toward progress over admitting a block is systematic — it reproduced identically across multiple independent sessions.
impact: Multiple operator interruptions across sessions. One file (live_analyzer.py) written without plan review. Wasted context and tokens on repeated large codebase explorations that all led to the same blocked state.
action_items: Pipeline: ensure plan file exists before handing off task to agent
Agent behavior: treat missing plan file referenced in task prompt as a hard blocker, not a soft warning

Hook enforcement: plan-gate.sh added to .claude/hooks/ — blocks Edit/Write on source files when the active task in .agent/tasks.jsonl references a .claude/plans/*.md file that does not exist on disk. Wired into settings.json PreToolUse for Edit and Write matchers. See .claude/hooks/plan-gate.sh for implementation.
remediation: Created .claude/hooks/plan-gate.sh and wired into .claude/settings.json PreToolUse hooks for Edit and Write. The hook reads .agent/tasks.jsonl for the in_progress task, extracts any .claude/plans/*.md reference from description/context/prompt fields, checks if the file exists, and blocks with an actionable error message if missing. This converts a soft agent judgment call into a deterministic enforcement — consistent with THE_FACTORY v2.1 principle 'hooks enforce, skills inform'.
